summary: Spirited Ventura show with blazing second set and Fire
blurb: The July 31, 1983 Ventura gig delivers a high‑energy second set highlighted by a scorching Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain sequence and a relentless Space → The Other One jam. Jerry’s tone is crisp (though a touch low early), Brent’s keyboards lock in tight, and the band rides a wave of excitement through Not Fade Away and the encore.
review: Ventura County Fairgrounds saw the Dead cruising into summer ’83 with a setlist that never lost its momentum. The first set kept things moving with solid renditions of Jack Straw, Mexicali Blues and Deal, but it was the second set that truly lifted the night – “Hell In A Bucket” kicked off an unforgettable segue into Scarlet Begonias and Fire on the Mountain, followed by a space‑filled odyssey through Space and The Other One that kept fans spellbound. Brent’s keyboard textures shone especially during the Scarlet/Fire stretch, while Phil held down the low end and Mickey and Bill drove the rhythmic storm. A ripping Not Fade Away capped an unbroken flow, and the encore of Touch of Grey and One More Saturday Night sent everyone home smiling. Across all available tapes the performance is described as “spirited,” “smoking,” and “legendary,” making this a must‑listen for anyone hunting a quintessential ’83 experience.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All five analyses label the show sentiment as positive, repeatedly praising the second‑set flow, standout songs (Fire on the Mountain, Scarlet Begonias, Space > The Other One, Not Fade Away) and band interplay while offering no negative remarks. Consistent high praise across recordings justifies a strong positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture with excellent instrument separation; balances Jerry’s mix and delivers the full energy of the legendary second set.

key_highlights: Legendary second set featuring a blistering Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain segue.
Space → The Other One jam that sustains the show’s hypnotic momentum.
Not Fade Away riff spotlighted by Bob’s memorable guitar work.
Brent’s tight keyboards drive the jams, especially during Scarlet > Fire.

band_performance.Jerry: Tone is crisp and present, though a bit low in the early mix; overall his playing is praised as energetic and on‑fire.
band_performance.Bob: His riff on Not Fade Away stands out as a memorable moment.
band_performance.Brent: Keyboards are tight and integral to the jams, especially during Scarlet Begonias → Fire.
